project.name: MenuTidy
project.one_liner: Hide and organize noisy macOS menu bar icons with one hover.

gate_reason: Pain evidence is anecdotal (score 34) — no clustered, cited primary evidence yet. Keyword and content work is discounted until real user pain is documented.

dimensions.name: Pain evidence (primary)

evidence.excerpt: Founder: 'My own menu bar is a mess, I assume everyone hates it.'
One r/macapps thread: 'too many icons up top, is there a fix?' (7 upvotes)

gaps.summary: No clustered problem hypothesis from ≥5 interviews or 20+ reviews
Evidence is founder belief, not observed user pain

next_actions: Read 25+ reviews of competing menu-bar apps and cluster complaints
Run 5 short interviews with heavy menu-bar users

dimensions.name: User language & intent

evidence.excerpt: Described as 'menu bar manager utility' — developer framing.

gaps.summary: No dev-term ↔ user-term glossary; no intent classification

next_actions: Build a glossary: 'menu bar manager' ↔ 'hide menu bar icons' / 'clean up top bar'
Tag top phrases by intent (informational vs solution-seeking)

dimensions.name: Keyword opportunity

evidence.excerpt: 'hide menu bar icons mac' looks low-competition per manual check.

gaps.summary: Scored on hunch, not the opportunity formula; capped by weak user-language map

next_actions: Score candidates by intent × relevance × 1/competition × product_fit after glossary exists

dimensions.name: SERP / competition

evidence.excerpt: Top results are two established apps + one stale 2021 listicle.

gaps.summary: Stale listicle is beatable but no content planned to fill the gap

next_actions: Draft a 2026 'best menu bar apps' comparison to displace the stale listicle

dimensions.name: Channel selection (1–2 deep)

evidence.excerpt: Plan is 'post on Twitter, TikTok, Reddit, and Product Hunt'.

gaps.summary: Four channels = shallow effort; not narrowed to 1–2 by intent fit

next_actions: Narrow to Google Search (high intent) + Reddit (trust) and go deep

dimensions.name: Directory strategy

evidence.excerpt: Comparison intent exists ('best menu bar apps'); single product only.

gaps.summary: Portfolio fit low (1 product) → third-party listings, not owned directory

next_actions: Identify 3–5 trusted Mac-app directories that already rank and list there

dimensions.name: Content assets & format

gaps.summary: No content produced yet; format for niche unresearched

next_actions: Produce 1 before/after demo GIF and 1 comparison post (pain-first, not feature-first)

dimensions.name: Landing page / on-page SEO

evidence.excerpt: Landing headline is 'Advanced menu bar management' — feature framing.

gaps.summary: Headline doesn't match user search intent/language

next_actions: Rewrite headline to user language: 'Clean up your Mac menu bar in 10 seconds'

dimensions.name: App Store Optimization

evidence.excerpt: Subtitle unused; keyword field has brand terms only.

next_actions: Add user-language keywords to subtitle + keyword field; lead screenshots with the before/after

dimensions.name: GEO / AI visibility

evidence.excerpt: No llms.txt; not cited in any AI-recommended lists.

gaps.summary: Brand absent from curated lists AI tools cite

next_actions: Add llms.txt; earn inclusion in comparison lists Perplexity/ChatGPT cite

dimensions.name: Conversion / activation / retention

evidence.excerpt: Tracks installs only; no first-value or retention metric.

next_actions: Define first successful action (first icon hidden) and measure time-to-first-value

dimensions.name: Measurement & learning loop

gaps.summary: No attribution from activation back to keyword/channel/content

next_actions: Add UTM + event pipeline so activation attributes to source; set a monthly repeat/adjust/drop review

channel_recommendation.directory_call: Track B: strategic third-party listings (single product — owned directory not yet justified)
channel_recommendation.rationale: High purchase intent lives in Google Search; Reddit (r/macapps) is where target users show pain and trust peers. Narrow from four channels to these two and go deep before expanding.

red_flags.assumption: Users have problems, so they will search
SEO content should be built around keywords

top_actions.summary: Read 25+ competitor reviews and cluster complaints into named problem hypotheses
Run 5 short interviews with heavy menu-bar users
Narrow from four channels to Google Search + Reddit and go deep
Rewrite landing headline in user language ('Clean up your Mac menu bar in 10 seconds')
Build dev-term ↔ user-term glossary with intent tags
